title: Support windows backslash paths
body:
Thanks for taking the time to report this.

I'm getting xmake to support verilator, https://github.com/xmake-io/xmake/issues/3257#issuecomment-1382728743

I've got it working successfully on macos/linux, but I'm having some problems on windows, but I don't know what's causing the problem.

Can you attach an example that shows the issue?  (Must be openly licensed, ideally in test_regress format.)

main.v

```
module hello;
  initial begin
    $display("hello world!");
    $finish ;
  end
endmodule
```

```console
verilator --cc --make cmake --prefix test --Mdir C:\Users\ruki\AppData\Local\Temp\.xmake\230114\_A1905FCCCD594F708649E5D19828E0C0.dir C:\Users\ruki\AppData\Local\Temp\.xmake\230114\_A1905FCCCD594F708649E5D19828E0C0.dir\main.v

%Error: Cannot find file containing module: C:\Users\ruki\AppData\Local\Temp\.xmake\230114\_B16190110D014270819F09DD1F59C8C0.dir\main.v
%Error: This may be because there's no search path specified with -I<dir>.
        ... Looked in:
             C:\Users\ruki\AppData\Local\Temp\.xmake\230114\_B16190110D014270819F09DD1F59C8C0.dir\main.v
             C:\Users\ruki\AppData\Local\Temp\.xmake\230114\_B16190110D014270819F09DD1F59C8C0.dir\main.v.v
             C:\Users\ruki\AppData\Local\Temp\.xmake\230114\_B16190110D014270819F09DD1F59C8C0.dir\main.v.sv
             C:\Users\ruki\AppData\Local\Temp\.xmake\230114\_B16190110D014270819F09DD1F59C8C0.dir/C:\Users\ruki\AppData\Local\Temp\.xmake\230114\_B16190110D014270819F09DD1F59C8C0.dir\main.v
             C:\Users\ruki\AppData\Local\Temp\.xmake\230114\_B16190110D014270819F09DD1F59C8C0.dir/C:\Users\ruki\AppData\Local\Temp\.xmake\230114\_B16190110D014270819F09DD1F59C8C0.dir\main.v.v
             C:\Users\ruki\AppData\Local\Temp\.xmake\230114\_B16190110D014270819F09DD1F59C8C0.dir/C:\Users\ruki\AppData\Local\Temp\.xmake\230114\_B16190110D014270819F09DD1F59C8C0.dir\main.v.sv
%Error: Exiting due to 2 error(s)

```

What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?

 rev v5.004-58-g5fce23e90 (mod)

What OS and distribution are you using?

Windows 7

May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?

sure
